summary.en: Clayton Christensen's theory that distinguishes sustaining innovation (better products for existing high-end customers) from disruptive innovation (initially-inferior products serving overshot low-end or non-consumption customers that incumbents rationally ignore — until the new entrant moves upmarket).
summary.zh: 克莱顿·克里斯坦森的理论：把维持性创新（给现有高端客户做更好的产品）与颠覆性创新（一开始性能更差，先服务被过度满足的低端用户或不消费用户，在位者出于理性而忽视——直到新进入者向高端移动）区分开。

sources.label: Clayton M. Christensen · The Innovator's Dilemma · Harvard Business School Press · 1997
Clayton M. Christensen, Michael E. Raynor & Rory McDonald · What Is Disruptive Innovation? · Harvard Business Review · December 2015

references.label: Clayton M. Christensen · The Innovator's Dilemma · Harvard Business School Press

note.en: Origin source for the disruptive vs. sustaining distinction, the technology S-curve, and the incumbent's rational-failure mechanism.
note.zh: 颠覆性 vs 维持性的区分、技术 S 曲线，以及在位者「理性失败」机制的原始来源。

references.label: Christensen, Raynor & McDonald · What Is Disruptive Innovation? · Harvard Business Review

note.en: Christensen's own clarification 18 years later: low-end vs new-market disruption, and the strict criteria the theory imposes on the label.
note.zh: 克里斯坦森在 18 年后亲自澄清：低端颠覆 vs 新市场颠覆，以及理论对「颠覆」标签施加的严格判定标准。
